Article Title: Ac Price Hikes: Facing cost pressure, AC cos signal price hikes
Article Text:
ANI file photo

NEW DELHI: India's room air-conditioner market is heading into the summer season under clear price pressure, even as companies remain bullish on long-term growth. Manufacturers including Blue Star have indicated further price hikes, driven by rising commodity costs, tighter energy-efficiency labelling norms that came into effect in Jan 2026 and current global headwinds. "The increase in commodity prices has been unprecedented," said B Thiagarajan, managing director of the company.Industry executives estimate that higher input costs have added roughly 8-9% to material expenses, while the shift to more energy-efficient models is pushing prices up by another 5-8%. Together, this has translated into a 13% increase in AC prices, said Thiagarajan. However, a GST reduction has softened the blow, with consumers likely to see an effective rise of about 5% compared to last year.Companies have already passed on part of the increase, with more expected in phases. "A large part of the increase is already behind us. The balance will be taken as the season progresses," Thiagarajan said, indicating a staggered pricing strategy. Other players, including Godrej Enterprises Group, have also raised prices. "Despite a challenging year for the AC industry, which witnessed a decline in overall sales, Godrej ACs achieved significant growth," said Kamal Nandi, business head, appliances business at the company.Godrej has increased AC prices by nearly 10%.At Hisense India, CEO Pankaj Rana said the recent price hikes are a function of sustained input cost pressures. "The recent increase in AC prices is primarily driven by a sharp rise in input costs, including raw materials, logistics, and broader supply chain disruptions," he said.Rana noted that companies have ensured adequate inventory and strong on-ground presence to meet summer demand. "While demand remains robust, any further price revisions will depend on how input costs evolve, as well as the competitive landscape, with potential increases in the range of 7-10%, depending on demand trends and competitive responses," he said.Manufacturers are walking a tightrope between protecting margins and sustaining demand during peak summer months. Apart from commodity inflation, elevated copper prices, a weaker rupee, and higher freight and component costs continue to weigh on profitability. Demand, meanwhile, has been volatile due to erratic weather and unseasonal rains, which impacted peak-season sales last year. Thiagarajan said 2025 turned out to be a weak summer, with industry volumes likely declining 5-7.5% compared to FY25.The outlook, however, remains strong. The domestic AC market, estimated at 14 million units, is expected to nearly double to about 30 million units by FY30, driven by rising incomes, heat, and deeper penetration in smaller towns.
Article Summary: India's room air-conditioner market is heading into the summer season under clear price pressure. Manufacturers including blue star have indicated further price hikes. Higher input costs have added roughly 8-9% to material expenses. The shift to more energy-efficient models is pushing prices up by another 5-8%.

Article Title: TRP reporting for TV news channels withheld for another 4 weeks
Article Text: NEW DELHI: The government has extended for four weeks its order to withhold the reporting of Television Rating Points (TRPs) for TV news channels with the aim of "curbing display of sensationalism and speculative content" amidst the West Asia conflict.The ministry of information and broadcasting had, on March 6, issued the first directive to the television rating agency Broadcast Audience Research Council (BARC) to immediately withhold reporting the TRPs for TV news channels for a period of four weeks or until further orders, whichever is earlier."The said direction was aimed towards curbing the display of unwarranted sensationalism and speculative content by some news genre channels amidst the West Asia conflict, which may potentially create panic among the general public, especially those having friends and family in the affected areas or residing in affected areas," the fresh order from the Ministry said, adding the "conflict situation still largely prevails at the moment".In the public interest, the ministry said, it directs M/s BARC to withhold reporting the TRPs for news TV channels for a further period of four weeks or until further directions, whichever is earlier.According to the 'Policy Guidelines for Television Rating Agencies in India', issued by the ministry on January 16, 2014, for television rating, BARC is a registered television rating agency.Clause 24.2 of the policy guidelines prescribes that: "The company shall be required to comply with any orders, regulations, guidelines, directions, etc., issued by the ministry from time to time."
Article Summary: The government has extended for four weeks its order to withhold the reporting of television rating points (trps) for tv news channels. The aim is to "curbing display of sensationalism and speculative content" amidst the west asia conflict. The ministry of information and broadcasting had, on march 6, issued the first directive.

Article Title: Mumbai Bmc: Mumbai developers pour Rs 11,626 crore into BMC coffers in 2025-26, highest since Covid lockdown period
Article Text: MUMBAI: City builders were the top revenue source for the BMC in 2025-26, paying premiums and development charges totalling Rs 11,626 crore. This revenue generated due to large-scale construction activity, mainly redevelopment of buildings, far surpasses BMC's highest-ever collection from property tax (Rs 7,610 crore) in the last financial year."The development plan department is the biggest revenue earner for the municipal corporation," said a civic official.This is the highest ever money collected by the BMC from developers since the Covid-19 lockdown period. In October 2020, the BMC offered a 50% discount in payment of premiums for 15 months to boost the real estate industry. This bonanza helped the municipal corporation garner Rs 14,500 crore by the time the discount scheme ended in January 2022."The BMC's strong premium collections are closely linked to the availability and utilisation of additional floor space index (FSI) across key micro-markets. South Mumbai, where height restrictions are almost non-existent, and higher FSI can be effectively deployed, allow developers to build taller towers and unlock greater value," said Gulam Zia, international partner and senior ED, Knight Frank India.He added, "This, in turn, results in higher premium payments, especially as these are linked to elevated Ready Reckoner rates in such prime areas.In contrast, markets constrained by aviation norms or height restrictions see relatively lower premium flows due to limited development potential. The current collections reflect this concentration of high-value activity, and while they may moderate with any slowdown, the scale achieved this year remains noteworthy. "According to data provided to TOI by BMC's development plan department, the belt between Bandra and Jogeshwari brought the largest revenue income from developers (Rs 2,451 crore) followed by south and central Mumbai (Colaba to Byculla — Rs 2,392 crore). The belt between Goregaon and Dahisar recorded a collection of Rs 1,811 crore. Among the municipal wards, K-West ward (Andheri West) contributed the largest share in Greater Mumbai (Rs 301 crore) followed by H-West ward (Bandra-Khar-Santacruz — Rs 283 crore).According to data from Liases Foras, a non-broking real estate research firm, developer sales in Greater Mumbai touched Rs 1.32 lakh crore in 2025, registering an 8% growth over 2024."Data indicates that the Mumbai real estate market continues to maintain strong momentum, supported by healthy sales and increased development approvals, even as completion activity shows signs of moderation," said Pankaj Kapoor, MD, Liases Foras.
Article Summary: City builders were the top revenue source for the bmc in 2025-26, paying premiums and development charges totalling rs 11,626 crore. This is the highest ever money collected by the bmc from developers since the covid-19 lockdown period. The belt between bandra and jogeshwari brought the largest revenue income from developers.

Article Title: Jeff Bezos: As Jeff Bezos 'celebrates' post on first-ever purchase made on Amazon, Elon Musk replies, says: It was start of ...
Article Text:
Why Jeff Bezos chose to sell books on Amazon





Jon Erlichman, BNN Bloomberg contributor and the host of Ticker Take, a YouTube channel dedicated to investing and financial literacy recently shared photos of Amazon ’s first non-employee purchase. The X post included original packing slip and Douglas Hofstadter's "Fluid Concepts and Creative Analogies," a book exploring AI and computer models of thought. “On this day in 1995: the first item was purchased on Amazon. It was a book on artificial intelligence,” he captioned the post. Amazon founder and former CEO Jeff Bezos shared the post on this timeline with a smiling face emoji.The post caught the attention of Elon Musk who replied writing: “It was the start of something great”.For those unaware, Amazon was founded by Jeff Bezos on July 5, 1994 in Bellevue, Washington. Originally named ‘Cadabra’, it then operated as an online bookstore out of Bezos’ garage before expanding into an online commerce platform. Visitors to the website were then greeted with a message “Welcome to Amazon.com Books!" and the promise of being able to order from "one million titles".During the early days, Jeff Bezos ran the company along with his ex-wife MacKenzie Scott and seven other employees. Amazon celebrated its 30th anniversary last year. In a 1997 interview, Bezos revealed that he chose to sell books because there were more varieties of books than any other type of product.At that time, Bezos estimated there were about 3 million books in print, about half of which were in English."When you have that many items, you could literally build a store online that couldn't exist any other way," Bezos then said.Since then, the company has expanded to become one of the world’s largest online retailers, expanding businesses into groceries, web services, and entertainment. Amazon now has a market cap of 2.25 trillion.
Article Summary: Jon erlichman, bnn bloomberg contributor and the host of ticker take, a youtube channel dedicated to investing and financial literacy recently shared photos of amazon’s first non-employee purchase. The x post included original packing slip and douglas hofstadter's "fluid concepts and creative analogies," a book exploring ai and computer models of thought.

Article Title: Artemis II Images: NASA releases Artemis II mission images taken on iPhone 17 Pro Max
Article Text:
Image courtesy: NASA

How iPhones made it onto NASA’s Artemis II mission







When NASA's Artemis II spacecraft lifted off, it carried four astronauts, decades of engineering ambition and an extraordinary arsenal of cameras, smartphones and communications technology to document humanity’s return to lunar orbit in detail. NASA approved the use of personal smartphones aboard Artemis II, making it one of the first missions where astronauts have brought their own handsets into space. The astronauts onboard carried four units of iPhone 17 Pro Max.Now NASA has released a series of striking images captured on the iPhone 17 Pro Max during the Artemis II mission, marking the first time consumer smartphones have been officially used to document a crewed lunar mission. The photos, taken on April 2, 2026, show astronauts Reid Wiseman and Christina Koch aboard the Orion spacecraft, gazing back at Earth through its cabin windows.“NASA astronaut and Artemis II mission specialist Christina Koch peers out of one of the Orion spacecraft's main cabin windows, looking back at Earth, as the crew travels towards the Moon,” wrote NASA while sharing the image on Flickr.For the first time, astronauts aboard Artemis II are carrying personal smartphones—specifically four units of the iPhone 17 Pro Max.It sounds like a small detail, but getting those devices approved for spaceflight was anything but straightforward.The clearance process followed a strict four-phase protocol. First, the hardware is introduced to a safety panel. Then comes hazard identification—everything from moving parts to materials like glass that could shatter in microgravity. The third phase outlines mitigation strategies, and the final stage validates that those fixes actually work.Durability was a key factor. The iPhone 17 Pro Max uses Ceramic Shield 2, which Apple claims is tougher than any smartphone glass. But NASA’s concerns went beyond just breakability. Devices behave differently in microgravity—heat dissipation, battery stability, and even how they’re stored all matter.While the iPhone has drawn attention, NASA continues to rely on its traditional imaging equipment for mission-critical documentation. The crew is also using Nikon D5, Nikon Z9, and GoPro HERO4 Black cameras to capture high-resolution scientific and operational imagery. The iPhones complement these tools by providing accessible, everyday snapshots from the astronauts’ point of view.
Article Summary: Nasa has released a series of striking images captured on the iphone 17 pro max during the artemis ii mission. The photos, taken on april 2, 2026, show astronauts reid wiseman and christina koch aboard the orion spacecraft, gazing back at earth through its cabin windows.

Article Title: OnePlus Nord 6 to launch in India today: Expected price, features and where to watch the event live
Article Text:
OnePlus Nord 6 launch today livestream details

OnePlus Nord 6 expected features

Watch OnePlus Nord 5 and OnePlus Nord CE 5: Unboxing and first look OnePlus Nord 5 and OnePlus Nord CE 5: Unboxing and first look

OnePlus Nord 6 expected price

Poll What feature of the OnePlus Nord 6 excites you the most? 9000 mAh battery 50MP main camera Sustained 165 FPS gaming





OnePlus Nord 6 is set to launch in India today. The smartphone is claimed to be the “segment’s largest 9000 mAh battery, segment leading 165Hz display and imaging prowess.” OnePlus has already revealed features of the upcoming Nord 6. The handset will be powered by a Snapdragon processor, a 50MP Sony main sensor and a 165Hz 1.5K AMOLED screen. The company has previously revealed that the OnePlus Nord 6 will be “the first in its category to support sustained 165 FPS gaming in popular titles such as BGMI, Call of Duty Mobile and Free Fire”. Here’s what to expect from OnePlus Nord 6OnePlus Nord 6 will be announced at an event scheduled for later in the day today. The launch event will be livestreamed on the OnePlus India YouTube Channel on April 7, 2026 at 7PM IST. The smartphone will go on sale on April 9, 2026 at 12 noon IST on Amazon.in, oneplus.in, OnePlus Experience stores and Select stores.OnePlus Nord 6 will be powered by the Qualcomm Snapdragon 8s Gen 4(4nm) with a 1+3+4 architecture, featuring a Cortex-X5 core up to 3.2GHz and Adreno 835 GPU. It is claimed to deliver OnePlus-exclusive ‘PC-level’ sustained 165 FPS gameplay across AAA titles like BGMI, Call of Duty Mobile, and Free Fire/Free Fire MAX.Gaming is further enhanced with ‘Edge Tech’, including a Touch Reflex Chip (3200Hz touch sampling), a 6-axis gyroscope (±4000dps) for improved shot accuracy, and a Spatial Audio Booster for better directional awareness and faster reactions. Connectivity is driven by the G2 Wi-Fi “Range Rocket” chip for stronger performance in congested environments, along with 5G-Advanced support offering up to 3× faster speeds with partners like Reliance Jio.The device features the same 165Hz 1.5K AMOLED “Sunburst HDR”display as the OnePlus 15, with 1800 nits HBM, up to 3600 nits peak brightness, 2 nits minimum brightness, and 3840Hz PWM dimming for eye comfort. The screen also comes with Aqua Touch 2.0 for improved accurate touch response.OnePlus Nord 6 packs the segment’s largest 9000 mAh battery and supports 80W SUPERVOOC charging, Bypass Charging to reduce heat during high-FPS gaming or 4K recording, and 27W wired reverse charging. A quick 10-minute charge is claimed to provide up to 12 hours of Spotify playback with OnePlus earbuds like the Nord Buds 4 Pro.OnePlus Nord 6 has a 50MP Sony main sensor with 'Dual-axis' Optical Image Stabilization (OIS) and a 32MP high-resolution front camera. AI-powered creative editing tools include AI Portrait Glow, AI Eraser, AI Unblur, AI Perfect Shot, and more, allows users to instantly correct common photo issues such as dim/coloured lighting, unwanted photobombers, blurry subjects.The Nord 6 comes with dedicated Plus Key. In addition, Google Gemini can now connect with Mind Space to pull information and provide personalized suggestions based on what you’ve saved - all in a single prompt.OnePlus Nord 6 will be available in three colours: holographic Quick Silver, Fresh Mint, and low-Reflection Pitch Black.While the exact pricing will be revealed at the launch event today, rumours suggest that the handset is likely to be priced in Rs 35,000 to Rs 40,000 range.
Article Summary: Oneplus nord 6 is set to launch in india today. The smartphone is claimed to be the ‘segment’s largest 9000 mah battery, segment leading 165hz display and imaging prowess’ oneplus has already revealed features of the upcoming nord 6. The handset will be powered by a snapdragon processor, a 50mp sony main sensor and a 165hz 1.5k amoled screen.

Article Title: Kirklin Mack: Who is Kirklin Mack? YouTuber goes viral after convincing Adam Raby to rob fake bank, fans compare him to MrBeast
Article Text:
Who is Kirklin Mack? YouTuber goes viral after convincing Adam Raby to rob fake bank, fans compare him to MrBeast (Image via Getty)

Kirklin Mack plans full fake bank heist with Adam Raby using actors, staged FBI agents, and a shut down town

Fans compare Kirklin Mack to Jimmy Donaldson as viral success grows and X reactions call him next MrBeast









Kirklin “Mack” is suddenly everywhere online. The 26-year-old YouTuber dropped a bold video on April 3, 2026, called “I Convinced a Stranger to Rob a Bank.” Within days, it crossed 5.2 million views. The idea sounds risky at first, but the twist is what grabbed people. Nothing in the video was real, yet everything looked real. That mix pulled viewers in fast. Mack is not new, but he is not a daily uploader either. He has around 727,000 YouTube subscribers, and still, almost every long video he posts gets huge numbers. That includes videos like “I Survived a Plane Crash” and “I Survived 100 Hours Controlled by AI.” He also works behind the scenes with Jimmy Donaldson on the Amazon Prime show Beast Games as a co-creator, co-host, and editor. Because of this, many fans now feel he could follow a similar path.His reach goes beyond YouTube too. On Instagram, he has over 306,000 followers. One of his reels from September 17, 2024, crossed 47 million views. Now, with this new video trending, people are paying even closer attention.The viral video shows Mack convincing his old school friend, 26-year-old Adam Raby, to take part in what looks like a real bank robbery.But the truth is very different. The entire setup was planned.Mack turned a whole town into a film set. The “bank” was fake. The FBI agents were actors. Even the weapons and police chase were staged. Adam Raby believed everything was real during the moment, which made his reactions more natural on camera.At the start of the video, Mack clearly explained the idea to viewers. He said, “This stranger thinks we’re about to rob a real bank. But, he doesn’t know these people are all actors. This bank is a secret film set. And I even shut down this entire town, just to convince him all of this is real. How much crime will he commit for money? Let’s find out.”Later, in the video description, he also admitted the cost of the project. He wrote, “Shutting down an entire town was not the most financially intelligent decision I’ve made this year.” That line showed how big and expensive the idea really was.After the video went viral, fans quickly started talking about Mack’s future. Many compared him to Jimmy Donaldson, who is known for large-scale YouTube challenges.On April 5, 2026, an X user named @Matt_YT posted that Mack could be the “next MrBeast if he keeps this up.” That reaction shows how strongly people feel about his style. Big ideas, high risk, and strong storytelling are the main reasons behind these comparisons.At the same time, Mack’s link to Beast Games gives him an edge. He already understands how large productions work. That experience is now showing in his own videos.Meanwhile, in the world of MrBeast, another big moment happened recently. A $1 million challenge ended with streamer YourRAGE winning after beating 49 other creators. That kind of content is what made MrBeast famous, and now fans see similar signs in Mack’s work.
Article Summary: Kirklin mack's "i convinced a stranger to rob a bank" video went viral on april 3, 2026. The video shows mack convincing his old school friend, 26-year-old adam raby, to take part in what looks like a real bank robbery. But the truth is very different. Mack turned a whole town into a film set. The fbi agents were actors.

Article Title: Jake Dennis: “Oh dear God, who gave you a mic?”: Alexa Rendell reveals Jake Dennis’ reaction to her as a presenter
Article Text:
Alexa Rendell at Madrid E-Prix. (Image credit: Instagram)

Formula E presenter Alexa Rendell shares her favourite on-the-job memory



Alexa Rendell shares what it takes to break into motorsport media



Formula E’s digital presenter and pit lane reporter, Alexa Rendell, has revealed one of her favourite memories from her time in the paddock as a media presenter, and it involves Andretti driver Jake Dennis’ surprising reaction to seeing her with a microphone. She also shared what aspiring women in motorsport media need to do to break into the industry. It was her TikTok videos that first caught Formula E’s attention and earned her the opportunity to become a presenter.Alexa Rendell is now a familiar face in the paddock and has built a fan following of her own through her interactions with fans during race weekends. When asked about her favourite memory from her time in motorsport, Rendell recalled her first experience as a live presenter for Formula E.She told FormulaE.com in an interview, “I got to present the autograph session on Instagram in Monaco. The fact that I was finally being trusted with a mic, I loved every second of it. I do distinctly remember Jake Dennis looking up at me with a microphone. He’d never seen me as a presenter before, and he went, ‘Oh dear God, who gave you a mic?’ And I like to think that attitude has followed me throughout my career, ‘Oh goodness, who gave Alexa a mic to start asking all these questions?’”Alexa Rendell grew up watching Formula One and its commentary before making her breakthrough in Formula E as a presenter.Sharing career advice for those looking to follow a similar path, Rendell said: “Just start doing what you want to do. If you want to work in social media, make your own TikToks like I did. I remember when I applied for my first job in motorsport, I had the second or third most-viewed video under the Formula E hashtag on TikTok, and that was the first line in my cover letter. ”“If you want to work in communications or journalism, start writing - even if it’s only for your own blog. At least you’ll be able to show your enthusiasm and passion. Go and volunteer in grassroots motorsport, do whatever you can to prove that enthusiasm. It’s also really important to find out if this is definitely what you want to do - you don’t want to invest all your time and effort only to realise it’s not for you,” she added.It would be no surprise to see Rendell presenting at the upcoming Berlin double-header next month, scheduled from 1-3 May.Also read: Exclusive interview: Edoardo Mortara shines for Mahindra Racing this season, says “important to keep your feet on the ground”
Article Summary: Alexa rendell is formula e's digital presenter and pit lane reporter. She has revealed one of her favourite memories from her time in the paddock. Andretti driver jake dennis was surprised to see her with a microphone. She also shared what aspiring women in motorsport media need to do to break into the industry.

Article Title: Sebastien Buemi: Sebastien Buemi and Joel Eriksson join Envision Racing’s Race Against Climate Change in the Sundarbans
Article Text:
Envision Racing’s Sundarbans project gets boost from Sebastien Buemi and Joel Eriksson. (Image credit: Instagram)

Sebastien Buemi and Joel Eriksson join Envision Racing’s mission to plant 5 lakh trees in the Sundarbans



Envision Racing has launched a new initiative under its Race Against Climate Change mission. After planting thousands of trees in Madagascar, the team has shifted its focus to the vulnerable Sundarbans in India, where it aims to plant around five lakh trees to remove carbon dioxide and protect endangered species in the region. The two drivers have spoken about the initiative in a new video shared by the team.The video was shared by Envision Racing on its Instagram page with the caption: “Green light on the Sustainable Chain! Seb and Joel take you behind the scenes on our most ambitious mission yet. We’re only a quarter of the way to our goal, and we’re not lifting off the throttle until we hit 1 million trees planted. We’ve teamed up with Envision Energy India to protect the Sundarbans mangroves, linking your fan pledges directly to real-world impact. ”Sebastien Buemi and Joel Eriksson have both lent their voices to the Race Against Climate Change cause. Speaking about the mission, Buemi says in the video, “Sustainable Chain was launched back in 2021 as part of our Race Against Climate Change programme. Through this initiative, we encourage our fans to make more sustainable lifestyle choices to help the planet.”He and Joel go on to explain the initiative further, adding, “So far, our fans have made over 270,000 pledges.Each pledge is a commitment to make a lifestyle change that benefits the climate - for example, cycling to work. For every pledge, we planted a tree in Madagascar, meaning over 270,000 trees have been planted.”Buemi adds about the highly vulnerable Sundarbans region, “Our mission doesn’t stop here. We are now aiming to plant 500,000 trees in the Sundarbans. By planting mangrove trees, we will help remove CO2 from the atmosphere and protect endangered species.”Eriksson further explains how the initiative will also help provide livelihoods for local communities and improve access to fresh water in the region. Together, they add, “You can be part of this amazing project. Make a pledge on our website today and share it with a friend or family member. By doing this, you join the chain - the Sustainable Chain.”Buemi is currently ninth in the Formula E Drivers’ Standings with 43 points. He secured his first podium of the season in Jeddah (Round 5), finishing second. Eriksson is 11th with 19 points, with his best result being a fourth-place finish at the Miami E-Prix earlier this year.They will return to racing next month for the Berlin E-Prix, scheduled from 1-3 May.Also read: Exclusive interview: Edoardo Mortara shines for Mahindra Racing this season, says “important to keep your feet on the ground”
Article Summary: Envision racing has launched a new initiative under its race against climate change mission. The team has shifted its focus to the vulnerable sundarbans in india. It aims to plant around five lakh trees to remove carbon dioxide and protect endangered species. Sebastien buemi and joel eriksson have both lent their voices to the cause.

Article Title: Rasmr: “This is why content is so boring”: Streamer Rasmr backs Rakai in MrBeast controversy
Article Text:
Image Via Instagram

“50

Streamers

Fight for $1,000,000”

“uptight”

“villain”

Streamer Rasmr defends Rakai as villain in MrBeast video

“This is why content is so boring these days. Everybody’s so uptight, so nice and afraid to ruffle feathers.”

“You gotta respect Rakai for having the balls to be a villain in a MrBeast video in front of like 200,000,000 people.”







Online fans are talking a lot about what happened in MrBeast’svideo. Rakai, one of the streamers, played aggressively in the game and caused some arguments. Some viewers think he went too far, especially in a moment where El Rubius and YourRAGE felt they were being ganged up on. After Rakai was eliminated, he reportedly argued and refused to leave quietly, which made more people criticize him online.Now, popular streamer Rasmr is stepping in to defend Rakai. He believes Rakai is getting too much hate for just playing the game his way. Rasmr said that many online viewers are tooand that content today has become too safe. He thinks Rakai was brave to take on therole in such a big video watched by millions.Rasmr explained why he thinks Rakai’s actions should not be criticized so harshly. He said,He also added,Rasmr’s point is that most creators today avoid being too bold because they are afraid of online backlash.But Rakai acted differently, playing the game aggressively and embracing a villain role. For Rasmr, this makes him interesting and refreshing in a world where most videos feel too safe and scripted.Some fans still think Rakai went too far, but Rasmr and other supporters believe it is part of the show. Being a villain in a game like this does not mean someone is a bad person, it just makes the video more exciting. Rasmr is using this moment to talk about how the creator space has changed and how bold personalities like Rakai are rare today.In the end, Rakai’s actions in the MrBeast video created a lot of debate. While some viewers criticized him, others, including Rasmr, see him as someone who dared to break the usual safe content rules. For many fans, this makes Rakai a memorable part of the $1,000,000 streamer challenge.
Article Summary: Streamer rasmr defends rakai as villain in mrbeast video. Rasmr believes rakai is getting too much hate for just playing the game his way. Some fans still think rakai went too far, but rasmr and other supporters believe it is part of the show.

Article Title: Quote Of The Day By Bill Gates: Quote of the day by Bill Gates: “I choose a lazy person to do a hard job. Because a lazy person will find an easy way to do it.”
Article Text:
Quote of the day by Bill Gates

Quote of the day by Bill Gates

“I choose a lazy person to do a hard job. Because a lazy person will find an easy way to do it.”

Meaning of the quote by Bill Gates

The thinking behind this approach

Efficiency in real-world work environments

How this quote applies to daily life

Why the quote is still relevant today

Other well-known quotes by Bill Gates

“Success is a lousy teacher. It seduces smart people into thinking they can’t lose.”

“Your most unhappy customers are your greatest source of learning.”

“Don’t compare yourself with anyone in this world. If you do so, you are insulting yourself.”

“We always overestimate the change that will occur in the next two years and underestimate the change that will occur in the next ten.”









Most people think that hard work means putting in a lot of time and effort. People often think that the only way to get things done is to work hard. But a famous quote by Bill Gates makes you think differently. He said, “I choose a lazy person to do a hard job." Because a lazy person will find an easy way to do it. This statement may sound strange or even wrong at first. But when you put it in the right context, it shows a useful way of thinking that is used in business, technology, and everyday problem-solving. The quote shows how important it is to be efficient instead of putting in extra work. This idea is very important in many fields today because time and productivity are so closely linked. By prioritizing clever shortcuts over brute force, we unlock innovative solutions that maximize output while minimizing the exhaustion of resources.The quote is about being efficient. In this case, "lazy" doesn't mean someone who doesn't want to work or take care of their responsibilities. It actually means someone who doesn't want to put in extra work and looks for easier ways to get things done.When given a hard task, that person is more likely to think differently.They try to cut down on the number of steps in a long and complicated process. This often results in quicker and better solutions.To put it simply, the quote means that people who don't like to waste time tend to come up with better ways to do things. These methods can help not only themselves but also other people do their jobs better. A lot of people use this method in fields where making things more efficient is important.The idea shows a change from working harder to working smarter. In a lot of cases, doing the same thing over and over again doesn't make things better. How the task is done is more important.For instance, in technology, developers often try to make things easier. They want to make systems that are shorter and more effective instead of writing long, complicated instructions. This cuts down on mistakes and saves time.The same thing happened with Microsoft, the company that Bill Gates helped start. Early software systems were hard to use, but over time, they were made to be easier for users. This change happened because developers wanted to make things easier and less work.In the workplace, getting things done quickly is often more important than how long it takes to do them. Employers want to hire people who can do their jobs correctly and quickly.For instance, in an office, someone who organises their work well can get things done faster than someone who works all the time without a clear plan. In the same way, businesses use machines and software to do repetitive tasks so that people can do more important ones.This shows that less work doesn't always mean less productivity. It often leads to better results.This quote doesn't just apply to work or technology. You can also see it in everyday life.People often try to find easier ways to do things they do every day. For example, using a simple tool instead of doing something by hand can save you time and energy. These little changes add up over time to make everyday life more efficient.This method also helps you make choices. People can find clearer and more useful solutions when they focus on making problems easier to understand.Efficiency is now very important in almost every area of life. Companies want to cut costs, speed things up, and get more done. Technology keeps getting better at making things easier and faster.The idea behind automation, artificial intelligence, and digital tools is to make things easier while still getting the same results. This is directly related to the quote's meaning.This is why the statement is still true today. It shows a way of thinking that encourages new ideas and finding real-world solutions.
Article Summary: Quote of the day by bill gates is about being efficient. The quote shows how important it is to be efficient instead of putting in extra work. People often try to find easier ways to do things they do every day. In the workplace, getting things done quickly is often more important than how long it takes.

Article Title: Popular American VC Marc Andreessen to all companies blaming AI for layoffs: You are ‘lying’ as …
Article Text:
Number of open tech roles in 2026 jump about 30%





Popular VC Marc Andreessen has once again stated that “AI job loss” narratives are fake arguing that AI will lead to massive productivity gains which in turn will boost demand and create a large jobs boom. “The "AI job loss" narratives are all fake. AI = massive ramp in productivity = massive ramp in demand = massive jobs boom. Watch,” Andreeseen writes in an X (formerly known as Twitter) post. He quoted a Business Insider article which said that tech openings have rebounded sharply in 2026, “challenging popular narrative that AI is wiping out engineering roles”.“1. Employer recovery from post-COVID hiring correction2. Employer recovery from post-COVID interest rate spike3. Elasticity = demand boom QED,”the VC wrote in another post, challenging interpretations that AI is not displacing engineering jobs, instead framing the increase as QED (quod erat demonstrandum) evidence of cyclical economic recovery rather than proof against AI's labor market effects.The report cited data from TrueUp, a tech hiring analytics firm, which showed more than 67,000 software engineering job openings, the highest level in over three years. As per the data, listings have roughly doubled since a trough in mid-2023."A lot of the 'AI is replacing engineers' narrative isn't grounded in job posting data — at least not so far," Amit Taylor, founder of TrueUp, told the publication.The TrueUp analysis data from late 2022 to 2026. The data showed a steep correction in 2022 and early 2023, when tech companies reduced hiring after over-expanding during the 2020 covid-19 pandemic boom.From mid-2023, hirings began to see an increase, reaching the current 67,000 software engineering job openings in present times.The trend seems contradictory with lesser jobs, especially for recent graduates. Explaining why, Taylor told BI: “Way more people have pursued computer science,” adding “The jobs haven't disappeared, but competition for them is dramatically higher than it was even five years ago.”“Maybe AI compresses some roles entirely. Or maybe it makes great engineers so leveraged that companies fight even harder over them,” Taylor said. “Right now, the demand for top talent is strong, but maybe that continues for a while until things suddenly flip.”
Article Summary: Number of open tech roles in 2026 jump about 30% from mid-2023 to 67,000. Andreessen: ai will lead to massive productivity gains which in turn will boost demand and create a large jobs boom. "a lot of the 'ai is replacing engineers' narrative isn't grounded in job posting data — at least not so far," amit taylor, founder of trueup, told bi.

Article Title: Mustafa Suleyman: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman: For the next couple years at least, entire AI industry is going to be defined by...
Article Text:
Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman argues that the AI industry's future hinges on companies that can afford to run models at scale, not just build the smartest ones. He posits that inference compute scarcity will define winners and losers for the next few years, with high-margin products gaining a significant advantage through a data flywheel of continuous improvement and adoption.

Why inference compute, not AI model training , is the real bottleneck in 2026

How Mustafa Suleyman's AI 'flywheel' gives high-margin products a compounding edge

Watch Microsoft CEO 'Thrilled' About India's Growing Data Centre Capacity, Details Meet With PM Modi Microsoft CEO 'Thrilled' About India's Growing Data Centre Capacity, Details Meet With PM Modi

Consumer AI apps and low-margin AI startups face a token rationing problem

Poll Which type of AI applications do you believe will struggle the most due to token rationing? Consumer AI apps Enterprise solutions Open-source models





Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman says the AI industry's next chapter won't be written by whoever builds the smartest model. It'll be written by whoever can afford to run one at scale. And right now, that's a very short list. In a post on X, Suleyman laid out a sharp, economics-first thesis—arguing that inference compute scarcity, not model intelligence, will define winners and losers for the next two to three years. The companies with the margins to buy tokens pull ahead. Everyone else gets rationed out."For the next couple years at least, the entire AI industry is going to be defined by this fact: demand is going to wildly outstrip supply, and so what matters is which companies / products have margin to pay for tokens," he wrote. The products that can pay, he added, will improve fastest—because lower latency drives retention, retention generates data, and that data spins a flywheel of model improvement and adoption.Suleyman's argument flips the dominant AI narrative. For years, the industry obsessed over training bigger foundation models. But the acute crisis in 2026 is on the serving side—running those models for millions of users in real time.Inference workloads now eat up roughly two-thirds of all AI compute spending, per Deloitte's 2026 TMT Predictions. GPU lead times have stretched to nearly a year. High-bandwidth memory from major suppliers is sold out through 2026. And of the 16 GW of global data-centre capacity slated for this year, only about 5 GW is actually under construction—the rest remains announcements on paper.This scarcity is where Suleyman's flywheel logic takes over. Products with fat gross margins—enterprise legal tools, healthcare SaaS, Microsoft 365 Copilot—can absorb premium inference costs. That buys them lower latency. Lower latency keeps users coming back. Returning users generate rich, proprietary workflow data. That data fine-tunes and improves models. Better models drive more adoption and revenue. Repeat, faster each cycle.Suleyman has used this exact framing before—at the October 2024 IA Summit, he said the winners in vertical AI would be those who "nailed the fine-tuning loop" and got their data flywheel spinning. Microsoft's own numbers back it up: paid Copilot seats hit 15 million in Q2 FY2026, up 160% year-on-year, though still just 3.3% of the 450 million M365 commercial user base.The uncomfortable corollary is that consumer AI apps and cash-strapped startups face a squeeze. Without the margins to buy premium inference, they get slower responses, weaker retention, and a flywheel that never starts spinning.Some in the thread pushed back—arguing intelligence-per-dollar matters more, or that open-source and on-device models could crash inference costs entirely. But Suleyman's bet is clear and well-funded. With Microsoft pouring over $80 billion a year into AI infrastructure, he's banking on the idea that for the next couple of years, the business that can pay for tokens wins the intelligence race first.
Article Summary: Microsoft ai ceo mustafa suleyman argues that the ai industry's future hinges on companies that can afford to run models at scale. He posits that inference compute scarcity will define winners and losers for the next few years. Products with fat gross margins can absorb premium inference costs.

Article Title: Netflix unveils ‘VOID’, an AI model that can change a movie plot
Article Text:
Netflix

How Netflix VOID works







Netflix has unveiled an AI model that can alter the scene. While this is nothing new, Netflix’s model not just remove objects from video, it understands what should happen next, essentially altering the causality. Most object-removal technology leaves a gap where something used to be and/ or fills it with a plausible-looking background. VOID goes a step further: after erasing an object from a scene, it calculates how the surrounding environment should realistically respond to its absence, and in the process it adjusts the behaviour of other elements in the frame accordingly.The model, called VOID, is a vision-language system designed to edit video footage with a level of contextual awareness that sets it apart from several existing tools.For example, imagine this scenario: A movie director has just wrapped up a multi-million dollar action sequence where the star drives straight into a fiery, head-on collision. The cars explode, debris scatters, and the scene is a wrap.But suddenly, there is request to change the scene: The character survives and drive off into the sunset instead. This would mean costly reshoots or millions spent on computer-generated graphics.Netflix’s VOID can alter the scene and digitally erase the collision, the second vehicle, the smoke, and the flying debris. It will then generate new, realistic footage of pristine pavement, making it look as though the main character simply drove down an empty, peaceful road.The AI’s ‘magic’ lies in its grasp of real-world physics. Let us understand this by a different example. If a video shows a person jumping into a pool and splashing water onto the deck, VOID can completely erase the person. It will then intelligently alter the water and the wet concrete, leaving behind a perfectly still, undisturbed pool as if nobody had ever jumped in.“To train the model, we generate a new paired dataset of counterfactual object removals using Kubric and HUMOTO, where removing an object requires altering downstream physical interactions. During inference, a vision-language model identifies regions of the scene affected by the removed object. These regions are then used to guide a video diffusion model that generates physically consistent counterfactual outcomes,” Netflix said.“Experiments on both synthetic and real data show that our approach better preserves consistent scene dynamics after object removal compared to prior video object removal methods,” the company added.Netflix isn’t keeping this powerful tool locked behind studio doors, and made the VOID model available to the public on the AI platform Hugging Face, allowing anyone to download and use it.While there are already several video-altering tools on the market, such as Runway, DiffuEraser, and ProPainter, the Netflix team claims VOID is vastly superior, as per The Register.
Article Summary: Netflix has unveiled an ai model that can alter the scene. While this is nothing new, netflix’s model not just remove objects from video, it understands what should happen next. Most object-removal technology leaves a gap where something used to be and/ or fills it with a plausible-looking background.

Article Title: AI data centers are causing 'stress' not just to tech companies, but also private insurers
Article Text:
Massive investments, growing risks



Unique challenges for data centre insurance companies





AI data centers are reportedly putting new pressure on the insurance industry. According to a CNBC report, experts warn that the rapid growth of these projects is becoming a “stress test” not just for tech companies, but also for private insurers and lenders. Consulting firm McKinsey estimates that global spending on data centers could reach $7 trillion by 2030. As costs rise, tech giants are increasingly relying on private equity, private credit and debt to fund these projects. The scale of investments is creating challenges for insurers.“When you put $10 to $20 billion plus in a single location, it creates capacity issues in the marketplace,” said Tom Harper, data center leader at insurance broker Gallagher as quoted in the CNBC report.He added that the sector has been a “real stress test” over the last few years, as insurers struggle to provide coverage for such large and complex projects. While these facilities are seen as high-quality assets, Harper said insuring them has become difficult due to their size and risk concentration.Private data center deals have crossed $10 billion regularly, with some reaching as high as $40 billion. Companies including Nvidia, Microsoft and BlackRock have already taken part in large investment deals.Rajat Rana, partner at Quinn Emanuel Urquhart & Sullivan, described the scale as unprecedented. “We’re talking about trillions of dollars, and almost going back to the same cycle where there’s almost no transparency about the financing structures — the scale is astronomical,” he said. He added that the boom in AI data centers could be “the largest peacetime investment project in human history.”Experts say the way these projects are financed is adding another layer of risk. Much of the funding is happening through complex and less transparent debt structures. Rana warned that this trend feels like “deja vu,” referring to past financial cycles. He said the lack of transparency could create risks for investors, insurers and pension funds.As stated in the CNBC report, data centers offer unique challenges due to their mix of real estate and advanced technology. Insurers are now designing custom policies to manage these risks. However, challenges remain. Large facilities are often located in areas prone to extreme weather, and they store expensive equipment, sometimes even before installation.Another key concern is the mismatch between the lifespan of data centers and the hardware they use. While data centers can last decades, GPUs — the chips powering AI — typically last around seven years. Rana described this as a “GPU debt treadmill,” saying, “This is almost like a treadmill that these AI data centers are running on.”He explained that companies may need to keep raising debt to upgrade infrastructure as technology evolves.
Article Summary: Private data center deals have crossed $10 billion regularly, with some reaching as high as $40 billion. Mckinsey estimates that global spending on data centers could reach $7 trillion by 2030. Tech giants are increasingly relying on private equity, private credit and debt to fund these projects.

Article Title: Clavicular Viral Controversy: "Trans women are.....": Clavicular’s viral moment with trans women sparks fresh conversation on internet culture
Article Text:
A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Instead of conflict, the exchange highlights shared influence and mutual awareness. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration rather than ownership.

Clavicular and trans women share a surprising realization about online trends







The video of Clavicular, a short-video producer, is going around the Internet, yet not the reason why. There is no yelling or over-the-top drama. It is a mere, everyday talk that seems almost novel nowadays, in the age of internet, which is overflowing with content. The scene is easy-going, the tone of the narrative is straightforward, and the characters appear more concerned with conversation than combat.The message that is being addressed is the flow of the conversation. It discusses internet culture, the way people communicate with one another and the place of trends. Rather than bringing out a debate, the encounter towards curiosity. It quietly brings attention to something that a lot of people who use the internet notice but don't often say out loud. People in different communities often have a bigger impact on each other than they think.The conversation begins with a confident and lighthearted comment from one of the trans women present. She says, "You know that trans women are like the OG lip masters, right? We are very talented in the program. Like, I do the peptides, I'm on Zeta." Her tone carries a mix of pride and humor, setting a relaxed vibe at the table.Clavicular responds in a way that keeps the mood steady but adds a different angle.He says, "That is funny because you guys were using a lot of the words in like the South Korean subreddits in like 2014." It’s not confrontational. If anything, it feels like an observation. He points to how certain words and ideas may have traveled across different online spaces over time.Another voice joins in and brings the moment full circle. "Guys, we're like the OGs. I saw a couple of posts about that. We're like copying each other." That line hits in a simple but honest way. It reflects something familiar. Online culture is rarely created in isolation. It grows through sharing, borrowing, and sometimes even unknowingly repeating.That's what makes the clip so powerful. It doesn't want to make a point or win an argument. It only shows a group of people talking, listening, and figuring something out together.This moment feels different in a space where viral content often leads to fights. It reminds people that conversations can be calm, thoughtful, and still important. And sometimes, that honest silence is what makes it work.
Article Summary: A viral clip featuring clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration rather than ownership.

Article Title: Why did DLive shut down? Twitch YouTube and Kick rival collapses after eight years leaving creators confused
Article Text:
Why did DLive shut down? Twitch YouTube and Kick rival collapses after eight years leaving creators confused (Image via Getty)

DLive and Felix “PewDiePie” Kjellberg partnership once gave the platform global attention



DLive shutdown statement asks users to withdraw coins and save content before full closure







DLive has announced its official shutdown after attempting to compete with Twitch and YouTube through its livestreaming platform. The company announced the news on April 7 2026 which they confirmed will shut down operations immediately and permanently. Creatives on the platform now face restrictions because they cannot stream or earn money or manage subscription services. The platform which provided users with blockchain and cryptocurrency rewards faced difficulties to expand its user base because established platforms like Kick and Twitch and YouTube already dominated the market. The platform received negative feedback from users which resulted in diminished audience numbers. The company now requires users to withdraw their digital coins and end their subscriptions and save their content before the complete shutdown which will occur in eight years.DLive experienced its most significant achievement of 2019 when popular YouTuber Felix Kjellberg joined the platform and promoted it. His support brought huge attention and helped DLive grow quickly for a short time. The platform stood out because it used blockchain technology through BitTorrent Inc.The platform used cryptocurrency to reward both its creators and viewers instead of using traditional advertising methods. At that time people found this concept to be exciting because it represented a fresh idea.However, things did not stay strong. After BitTorrent Inc. acquired DLive in 2019, the platform tried to push crypto-based features even more. But most regular users did not fully adopt this system. Many creators also found it harder to grow compared to bigger platforms.Over time, DLive started falling behind. It lacked strong discovery tools, stable income options, and a large viewer base. While Twitch and YouTube kept improving their systems, DLive could not match their pace.In its official statement, DLive clearly told users what to do next. The company said:“Thank you for your continued support of DLive. We regret to inform you that the platform will be ceasing operations. The top-up function, including subscriptions, has been discontinued effective immediately.” This means users can no longer add money or support creators through subscriptions.The company also shared that more updates about the shutdown process will come soon. It said:“We will continue to share updates regarding the platform’s wind-down timeline to ensure you remain informed throughout this transition.”At the same time, DLive asked creators to take quick action. They must withdraw their remaining coins, cancel any active subscriptions, and download their videos before access is gone.In its final message, the team thanked its community:“All staff would like to once again thank you for your support throughout the years. Thank you all for the incredible memories, the streams, and your unwavering support over the years. We’ll remember this for life.”It is also important to note that DLive faced controversy in the past. In 2021, a report by The Forward described the platform as a space where extremist creators were active. This affected its reputation and growth. Now, with strong competition and past issues, DLive has come to an end.
Article Summary: Dlive has announced its official shutdown after eight years. The company announced the news on april 7 2026 which they confirmed will shut down operations immediately and permanently. Creatives on the platform now face restrictions because they cannot stream or earn money or manage subscription services.

Article Title: Indian man appears in court charged with mowing down pedestrians in Derby
Article Text:
Sandhu Ponnachan



TOI Correspondent from London:







An Indian man, believed to be from Kerala, has been charged with driving a car into pedestrians in Derby on Saturday night, leaving seven people seriously injured.Sandhu Ponnachan (36), who moved to Derby in April 2025 and is an Indian citizen, appeared at Southern Derbyshire magistrates’ court on Wednesday where he was charged with six counts of grievous bodily harm (GBH) with intent, one count of attempted GBH with intent, one count of dangerous driving and one count of possession of a bladed article. He spoke only to confirm his name and address and did not enter a plea. He was remanded in custody to appear at Derby crown court on April 29. There was no application for bail.Four men and three women, aged 36 to 52, had to be hospitalised after a black car mounted the pavement and ploughed into pedestrians at 9.30pm local time on March 28. Ponnachan was arrested on suspicion of attempted murder shortly afterwards in a visibly damaged car. Four people have since been discharged from hospital.His LinkedIn profile shows he was looking for work as a logistics manager, having worked in this role for Caterpillar Inc until March 2026.He was qualified to operate a forklift and work as a security guard. He is also listed as a director of Greeshmam Ltd.On Facebook Ponnachan has put up posts praising India’s rise as a great power and a video asking “Why did Kerala go bankrupt?” as well as posts in Malayalam. There are religious posts, including a photo showing Verse 10 of Śrī Rūpa Gosvāmī’s Upadeśāmṛta (Nectar of Instruction); a video of a painting of Lord Hanuman and of the Bhagavad Gita, and a repost of a post by Congress politician Shashi Tharoor showing the Ram Lalla idol at Ram Mandir. One repost blames the Congress-led UPA regime for the “mysterious deaths of India’s nuclear scientists”.The revelation of his nationality has triggered a barrage of racist abuse at Indians on social media, leading to the Friends of India Society International UK issuing a statement urging people not to let the actions of one person stigmatise an entire community. Users wrote on X: “Mass Immigration has been a disaster for this country. We need deportations.” Another wrote: “Annexing India and restoring the Raj will protect the world against India. ” Another wrote: “You shouldn’t be surprised. Look what Indians are doing to Canada. They are a crime syndicate.”
Article Summary: Sandhu ponnachan (36) moved to derby in april 2025 and is an indian citizen. He appeared at southern derbyshire magistrates’ court on wednesday. He was remanded in custody to appear at derby crown court on april 29.

Article Title: Kuwait Digital Payments: Kuwait sets KD10 cash limit across 6 service sectors, mandates digital payments above cap
Article Text:
Kuwait sets 10-Dinar cash limit across 6 service sectors/Image: Getty Images







Kuwait has introduced new rules limiting the use of cash in several service industries, with payments above 10 Kuwaiti dinars now required to be made through electronic or banking channels.The decision was issued by the Ministry of Commerce and Industry under Minister Osama Boodai, as part of efforts to improve financial transparency and regulate payment practices across the country.The restriction applies to a range of businesses, including health institutes, men’s and women’s salons, children’s salons, sports clubs, as well as companies involved in pest and rodent control. It also covers activities linked to the import, export, and storage of public health pesticides.Under the new regulation, any transaction exceeding 10 dinars must be processed through approved banking systems or electronic payment methods recognised by the Central Bank of Kuwait. Cash payments will only be permitted for amounts below this threshold.Authorities said the move is aimed at strengthening oversight of financial transactions, reducing reliance on cash, and encouraging the wider adoption of secure digital payment systems within regulated sectors.The decision forms part of broader efforts to align business practices with financial regulations and improve accountability in day-to-day commercial transactions.
Article Summary: Kuwait sets 10-dinar cash limit across 6 service sectors. Cash payments will only be permitted for amounts below this threshold. Move is aimed at strengthening oversight of financial transactions, reducing reliance on cash, and encouraging the wider adoption of secure digital payment systems within regulated sectors.
